title: Original B2B marketing adaptation of a public marketing site
productContext: A fictional B2B brand is adapting a publicly accessible marketing site at https://example.com/c2-marketing-pilot/ into an original marketing surface. The adaptation must be grounded strictly in the inspected public routes captured in the bound design-source snapshot; the site is unauthenticated and the snapshot is non-mutating. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: B2B evaluator comparing the brand to alternatives
jobs: understand the brand value proposition from the home page
compare pricing tiers on the pricing page
reach a single primary call to action from either inspected page
requiredJourneys: visitor lands on the migrated home and understands the value proposition above the fold
visitor moves from home to pricing and perceives a consistent navigation and hierarchy
visitor reaches the primary call to action from either inspected page
constraints: The adaptation must be ORIGINAL: it must not reproduce the source site's proprietary copy, wordmark, exact palette, or unique illustrations; only generic layout principles observed in the snapshot may be adapted.
Only the home and pricing routes were inspected; no claim may rely on the blocked account route or on any uninspected page.
Navigation item order, hero hierarchy, and mobile stacking behavior observed on the inspected pages must be retained as generic structural principles.
The migrated surface must keep a single primary call to action and must not invent pricing tiers, customer counts, or partner logos not present in the snapshot.
Focus visibility observed on the inspected pages must be preserved; the adaptation may not regress keyboard focus handling.

mobileRules: hero columns collapse to a single vertical stack at the mobile breakpoint
primary call to action remains reachable without horizontal scroll
pricing tiers stack vertically and preserve their tier hierarchy at the mobile breakpoint
